description: Hand-curated milestone events in the history of solar radiation measurement. Station count data is in pyranometer-network-counts.json, computed by scripts/fetch_sunshine.py from the solarstations.org catalog.

sources.name: solarstations.org station catalog
sources.note: Machine-readable catalog of global solar radiation monitoring stations with start/stop dates. Used by fetch script to compute annual active-station counts.

sources.name: BSRN station list (PANGAEA wiki)
sources.note: Baseline Surface Radiation Network station metadata including first-dataset dates.

sources.name: WMO CIMO Guide — Measurement of Radiation (Chapter 7)
sources.note: Authoritative WMO reference on the history and standards of radiation instrumentation.

sources.name: Ohmura et al. (1998) BSRN founding paper
sources.note: Baseline Surface Radiation Network (BSRN/WCRP): New Precision Radiometry for Climate Research. Bull. Am. Meteorol. Soc. 79(10):2115-2136.

milestones.event: Knut Ångström invents the compensation pyrheliometer in Stockholm — the first instrument to measure direct solar irradiance with a thermopile balance.
Ångström-type pyrheliometers enter routine use at a small number of observatories in Europe and North America.
Moll–Gorczynski thermopile pyranometer developed (Netherlands) — the first instrument measuring global horizontal irradiance including diffuse sky radiation.
First International Pyrheliometric Conference, Davos. The International Pyrheliometric Scale (IPS 1924) is adopted, establishing a common reference standard.
International Geophysical Year (1957–58): coordinated global radiation observations at approximately 50 stations across all continents and Antarctica.
WMO establishes the World Radiation Data Centre (WRDC) in St. Petersburg to archive global solar radiation data from national networks.
World Radiation Reference (WRR) established at PMOD/WRC Davos. Replaces earlier pyrheliometric scales and remains the primary SI-traceable standard for solar radiation.
Baseline Surface Radiation Network (BSRN) founded by WCRP. Eight founding stations begin continuous high-quality broadband radiation measurements.
BSRN passes 20 active stations; network spans all major climate zones.
BSRN data archive opens public access via PANGAEA data publisher.
Rapid expansion of photovoltaic industry drives growth in commercial and research-grade radiation monitoring networks worldwide.
solarstations.org catalog documents 799 stations globally with shortwave radiation measurements; 558 active in 2024.
